skill.name: design-system-patterns
skill.description: Build scalable design systems with design tokens, theming infrastructure, and component architecture patterns for web and mobile applications.
skill.summary: Create design tokens, theme systems, and reusable component architecture patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review found documentation examples, CSS values, TypeScript snippets, browser theme preference storage, and resource links. No executable scripts, command execution instructions, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings point to fenced TypeScript, CSS, JSON, and JavaScript tutorial examples. These files are Markdown guidance and do not execute commands or invoke shell APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown code fence starts or tutorial code blocks. No shell command API, process execution, or runtime skill script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Static High-Severity Keywords in Design Examples
low_findings.description: The high findings for weak crypto, C2 keywords, and certificate or key files are terminology matches inside design-system prose and UI code examples. They include color values, design token wording, keyboard event handling, and CSS class names, not cryptographic or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed ordinary design vocabulary and component examples at the cited lines. No encryption implementation, key material, beaconing, or C2 logic is present.

low_findings.title: Benign Browser Storage Examples
low_findings.description: The localStorage usage stores and retrieves a theme preference for light, dark, or system mode. It does not read credentials, tokens, or unrelated browser storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage key is used for theme selection examples only. There is no evidence of secret collection or network transmission.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links Only
low_findings.description: The network findings are static resource links to public documentation for design system libraries and specifications. The skill does not perform network requests or send user data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown links in Resources sections. No fetch call, webhook, credential parameter, or tracking endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Design systems often become inconsistent when tokens, themes, and components grow separately. This skill provides patterns for token hierarchies, theme switching, component APIs, and governance.
actual_capabilities: Explains primitive, semantic, and component token hierarchies.
Provides CSS custom property patterns for light and dark themes.
Shows React theme provider patterns with system preference support.
Documents component API patterns such as compound components and variants.
Outlines Style Dictionary outputs for CSS, iOS, and Android.
Describes governance practices for token changes and migrations.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a runnable design system package.
It does not inspect live Figma files or synchronize tokens automatically.
It assumes familiarity with frontend development and design token concepts.
Examples may need adaptation for each framework, brand, and accessibility standard.

use_cases.title: Create a Token Foundation
use_cases.description: Define token categories, naming rules, and platform outputs before building a shared UI library.

use_cases.title: Plan Theme Switching
use_cases.description: Implement light, dark, high contrast, and system themes with stable CSS custom property contracts.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component APIs
use_cases.description: Use compound components, variant systems, and accessibility patterns to make reusable components predictable.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Token Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a basic token hierarchy for colors, typography, spacing, and radii in a new web app.

prompt_templates.title: Theme Architecture Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding dark mode to an existing product.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my current CSS variables and propose a light and dark theme architecture with semantic tokens.

prompt_templates.title: Component Library API Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design component API patterns for buttons, accordions, badges, and form controls with variants and accessibility states.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Platform System
prompt_templates.scenario: Scaling a mature design system across platforms.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define a multi-brand token pipeline with Style Dictionary outputs, governance rules, and migration guidance.

output_examples.input: Plan design tokens for a SaaS dashboard.
output: A layered token model with primitive, semantic, and component tokens.
Naming rules for text, surface, border, spacing, and interaction tokens.
Guidance for versioning token changes and documenting usage.
A theme contract based on CSS custom properties.
Recommendations for system preference detection and stored user preference.
Checks for contrast, reduced motion, and forced color support.

output_examples.input: Design a reusable component library approach.
output: Component API recommendations for variants, sizes, slots, and composition.
Accessibility expectations for keyboard behavior and ARIA attributes.
Documentation topics for consumers and migration planning.

best_practices: Use semantic token names for intent, and keep raw values in primitive tokens.
Test every theme against real components, accessibility states, and platform outputs.
Treat token and component changes as versioned API changes with migration notes.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode colors and spacing inside components when tokens should express the decision.
Do not create theme-specific component branches when CSS variables can carry the theme contract.
Do not add variants without documenting usage, accessibility behavior, and design ownership.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete design system?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns, examples, and planning guidance that you adapt to your product and stack.

faq.question: Can it help with dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers CSS custom properties, React theme providers, system preference detection, and persistent theme choices.

faq.question: Does it support mobile design tokens?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes multi-platform token output concepts for CSS, iOS, and Android.

faq.question: Is Figma integration automatic?
faq.answer: No. It discusses Figma-to-code workflows and token pipeline structure, but it does not connect to Figma.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Design system engineers, frontend developers, and UI platform teams can use it to structure shared UI foundations.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No confirmed sensitive behavior was found. The reviewed risk indicators were documentation examples and public resource links.
